title: [CI:DOCS] Swap out javascript engine
body:
* Replace redoc with swagger javascript engine. redoc was causing an error. Only used when debugging swagger output.


Reported-by: Brent Baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *jwhonce* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [jwhonce] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
This helps to fix #22333 once we have a CI upgrade.  Thanks @jwhonce
Removed `kind/api-change` as there is no API change in this PR

Just adding for the record, the version bump on swagger is the other "part" of the equation here.  This should solve many of our frequent complaints about API docs.  If nobody objects, I'm also going to add the cherry-pick here so we can fix 5.0

@baude: once the present PR merges, I will cherry-pick it on top of v5.0 in a new PR and assign it to you.

This may have broken the site? #22351

Yes the change https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/22349/commits/1b6e8d73aa666ee3438e97f9d0d8258a71b88e08 broke the public site as it depend on redoc. And by the way that is a bad commit message, what is the error??? There is no linked bug or error message listed in the commit so it is impossible to verify for a reviewer why the change needed to be done.

> Just adding for the record, the version bump on swagger is the other "part" of the equation here. This should solve many of our frequent complaints about API docs. If nobody objects, I'm also going to add the cherry-pick here so we can fix 5.0

I do not see any change with this PR in regards to actually see the expected type information. Yes the format of the site was changed but the actual content is still missing for whatever reason.
